plan.title: installer-menu-manage-and-tag-selection
plan.goal: Enhance install.sh so it branches to install or manage when Agent Zero containers are already running, supports container management actions (start, stop, open in browser), and lets new installs choose a Docker Hub tag with latest as the default.

step.title: refactor-entrypoint-and-running-container-menu
step.goal: Convert the linear installer into function-based flow control and add the install/manage branch that appears only when running Agent Zero containers are detected.
step.context:
Repository root: /Users/nicolas/Code/agent-zero-fork Primary file to modify: install.sh
Current install.sh is a single linear script. Reuse these existing conventions: - Keep banner output and color constants at the top. - Keep helper output functions print_ok/print_info/print_warn/print_error. - Keep prompt style: printf + `IFS= read -r` + default assignment `${VAR:-default}`. - Keep POSIX sh compatibility (script shebang is `#!/bin/sh`, avoid bash-specific syntax).
Current Docker checks are in install.sh lines ~49-67: - docker existence check and Linux auto-install fallback - docker compose plugin presence check
New behavior required by product request: - After Docker checks pass, detect running Agent Zero containers. - If running containers exist, show two options: Install and Manage. - If no running Agent Zero containers exist, continue directly to install flow.
Use image family: `agent0ai/agent-zero`. Use a running-container detector command equivalent to: `docker ps --filter "ancestor=agent0ai/agent-zero" --format '{{.Names}}'`
This step should only scaffold flow and menu branching. Installation internals and management actions are implemented in later steps.
step.instructions:
Edit `install.sh` only.
1. Extract top-level logic into functions: - `check_docker()` for existing Docker + compose checks. - `count_running_agent_zero_containers()` that returns a numeric count. - `main_menu_for_existing()` that prompts when count > 0. - `main()` as entrypoint.
2. Implement branching behavior after `check_docker()`: - If running Agent Zero container count is greater than zero, print a short menu: 1) Install new instance 2) Manage existing instances - Route option 1 to `create_instance` and option 2 to `manage_instances`. - If input is empty or invalid, default to option 1. - If count is zero, call `create_instance` without showing the install/manage menu.
3. Add placeholder function stubs only if not yet implemented: - `create_instance()` - `manage_instances()` The stubs must print a clear temporary message and return success so the script remains executable.
4. Keep existing banner, colors, and logging style unchanged. 5. Do not modify any file except `install.sh`.
step.verification:
Run syntax check: `sh -n install.sh`
Check function and menu presence: `rg -n "check_docker|count_running_agent_zero_containers|main_menu_for_existing|create_instance\(|manage_instances\(" install.sh` `rg -n "Install new instance|Manage existing instances" install.sh`
Behavior check with mocked docker reporting one running container (menu must appear): - Put a mock `docker` executable first in PATH that returns success for `docker compose version` and emits one name for `docker ps --filter ancestor=agent0ai/agent-zero`. - Run `printf '2\n' | PATH="<mock_path>:$PATH" sh install.sh` and verify output contains manage-branch text.

step.title: implement-install-flow-with-dockerhub-tag-selection
step.goal: Implement the install path so users can pick an image tag from Docker Hub, with default latest when no selection is provided.
step.context:
Prerequisite output from previous step: - `install.sh` has `main()`, `check_docker()`, and branch routing to `create_instance()`. - `create_instance()` currently exists and is safe to replace from placeholder to full implementation.
User-requested behavior for install path: - Install always creates a new Agent Zero instance. - Include a tag-selection step before other install prompts. - Tags must be listed from Docker Hub API. - If user makes no tag selection, default to `latest`.
Docker Hub endpoint from install-plan.md: `https://registry.hub.docker.com/v2/repositories/agent0ai/agent-zero/tags/?page_size=15&ordering=last_updated`
Existing install prompts in install.sh should be preserved in style: - data directory - web UI port - auth login/password
Current compose generation block in install.sh writes: - service name `agent-zero` - container_name `agent-zero` - image `agent0ai/agent-zero`
To support "create new instance" safely when one already exists, install flow needs unique instance naming and per-instance compose file location. Follow install-plan.md pattern: - Instance directory: `~/.agentzero/<container_name>/` - Compose path: `~/.agentzero/<container_name>/docker-compose.yml` - Default data dir: `~/.agentzero/<container_name>/usr`
Do not add jq as a dependency. Use curl + python3 parsing fallback to lightweight text parsing if needed.
step.instructions:
Edit `install.sh` only.
1. Replace `create_instance()` placeholder with full flow: - Step A: tag selection. - Step B: container name (default `agent-zero`, auto-suggest next free name if taken, e.g. `agent-zero-2`). - Step C: data directory prompt defaulting to `~/.agentzero/<name>/usr`. - Step D: web UI port prompt (existing numeric validation style). - Step E: optional auth username/password prompts (existing behavior).
2. Add helper functions: - `fetch_available_tags()` to call Docker Hub API and emit tag list. - `select_image_tag()` to show numbered tags and resolve final tag. - Empty input must resolve to `latest`. - Invalid numeric input must fall back to `latest` with a warning. - API/network failure must still allow install and use `latest`.
3. Generate compose YAML per instance directory: - `container_name` must match chosen instance name. - `image` must be `agent0ai/agent-zero:<selected_tag>`. - Keep volume mapping to `/a0/usr` and auth env vars when provided.
4. Run install commands using explicit compose file path: - `docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" pull` - `docker compose -f "$COMPOSE_FILE" up -d`
5. Update final success summary to print selected tag, instance name, compose file path, and URL.
6. Do not modify files outside `install.sh`.
step.verification:
Run syntax check: `sh -n install.sh`
Confirm tag API and selected-tag compose usage in code: `rg -n "registry\.hub\.docker\.com.*/agent0ai/agent-zero/tags|fetch_available_tags|select_image_tag|agent0ai/agent-zero:\$" install.sh`
Smoke-test default tag behavior with mocks: - Mock `curl` to return a Docker Hub tags JSON containing at least `latest` and one version tag. - Mock `docker` to succeed for compose commands. - Run installer with empty tag choice and inspect generated compose file under a temporary HOME. - Verify compose contains `image: agent0ai/agent-zero:latest` when tag prompt is left blank.

step.title: implement-manage-flow-start-stop-open-actions
step.goal: Implement the manage path so users can select an existing Agent Zero container and run start, stop, or open-in-browser actions.
step.context:
Prerequisite output from previous steps: - `main()` routes option 2 to `manage_instances()`. - Install flow is fully implemented and supports multiple instance names.
Scope for management in this task (per user request): - User selects a container from Agent Zero containers. - Actions supported: Start, Stop, Open in browser. - Start/Stop should map directly to docker commands. - Open in browser should work for running containers by resolving host port and opening `http://localhost:<port>`.
Container list source: - Detect existing containers from image family `agent0ai/agent-zero`. - Prefer listing from `docker ps -a --filter "ancestor=agent0ai/agent-zero" --format ...` so stopped containers can be started.
Port resolution pattern: - `docker port <container_name> 80/tcp` returns host binding (example `0.0.0.0:5080`).
Cross-platform browser open helper in POSIX sh: - macOS: `open` - Linux: `xdg-open` (fallback to printing URL if unavailable)
Keep management UI prompt style consistent with existing installer prompts. Do not implement delete/restart/update/log actions in this task.
step.instructions:
Edit `install.sh` only.
1. Replace `manage_instances()` placeholder with full interactive flow: - List Agent Zero containers with index, name, image tag, and status. - Prompt for container selection by index. - Validate selection and handle empty/invalid input gracefully.
2. After selecting a container, show action menu: - 1) Open in browser - 2) Start - 3) Stop - 4) Back/Exit manage menu
3. Implement action handlers: - Open: resolve host port from `docker port <name> 80/tcp`, extract numeric host port, then call `open_browser "http://localhost:<port>"`. - Start: `docker start <name>`. - Stop: `docker stop <name>`. - If open is requested for a container with no resolvable port mapping, show warning and do not fail script.
4. Add `open_browser()` helper for Darwin/Linux fallback output. 5. Keep logs user-friendly with existing print_* helpers. 6. Do not modify files outside `install.sh`.
step.verification:
Run syntax check: `sh -n install.sh`
Confirm management commands are present: `rg -n "manage_instances|docker ps -a --filter \"ancestor=agent0ai/agent-zero\"|docker start|docker stop|docker port|open_browser" install.sh`
Behavior check with mocked docker/open commands: - Mock `docker ps -a` to return one running and one stopped Agent Zero container. - Mock `docker port` to return `0.0.0.0:5080` for the running container. - Run installer, choose manage path, select container, then run each action in separate runs. - Verify mocked command logs show expected calls (`docker start`, `docker stop`, and browser opener command or URL output).

step.title: final-regression-pass-and-user-flow-validation
step.goal: Verify the integrated script satisfies the requested UX and does not regress existing installer behavior.
step.context:
Prerequisite output from previous steps: - `install.sh` now includes install/manage branching, Docker Hub tag selection, and manage actions.
This final step is verification and polish only. Keep scope limited to `install.sh` messaging, defaults, and guardrails. No new features should be added here.
Requested UX requirements to validate end-to-end: - After Docker checks, if running Agent Zero containers exist, user sees Install vs Manage options. - Install path creates a new instance and includes tag selection from Docker Hub with default latest. - Manage path allows selecting an existing container and running start/stop/open.
The script should remain POSIX sh compatible and continue to work when Docker Hub is unreachable.
step.instructions:
Edit `install.sh` only if needed to fix issues found during verification.
1. Run end-to-end smoke tests for both branches using mocked commands and temporary HOME directories. 2. Fix any prompt-flow issues (invalid input loops, empty input defaults, branch routing errors). 3. Ensure fallback behavior is robust: - tag API failure -> latest - no containers in manage flow -> clear message and return to install or exit cleanly 4. Ensure the final summary for install flow reports instance name, selected tag, URL, and auth state. 5. Keep style consistent with existing print_* helpers and avoid introducing bashisms.
step.verification:
Required checks: 1) `sh -n install.sh` 2) `dash -n install.sh` (if dash is available) to catch non-POSIX syntax. 3) Branch smoke test (mock running container exists): menu offers Install and Manage. 4) Install smoke test with blank tag choice: generated compose file uses `agent0ai/agent-zero:latest`. 5) Manage smoke test: selecting start/stop/open executes expected docker/browser commands.
All checks must pass without modifying files outside `install.sh`.

criterion.title: Running-container menu appears only when applicable
criterion.requirement: When `docker ps --filter ancestor=agent0ai/agent-zero` returns at least one running container, the script shows Install and Manage options; when none are running, the script skips that menu and goes directly into install flow.

criterion.title: Install flow includes Docker Hub tag selection with latest default
criterion.requirement: The install flow queries Docker Hub tags, presents a selectable list, and uses `latest` when the user leaves the tag prompt empty or when tag fetch/parse fails.

criterion.title: New installs create isolated instances
criterion.requirement: Each install writes compose config to `~/.agentzero/<container_name>/docker-compose.yml` and uses `agent0ai/agent-zero:<selected_tag>` plus the configured port, data directory, and optional auth variables.

criterion.title: Manage flow supports required actions
criterion.requirement: The manage path lists Agent Zero containers, allows selecting one, and supports Open in browser, Start, and Stop actions with correct docker/browser command execution.

criterion.title: Installer remains POSIX-safe and executable
criterion.requirement: `sh -n install.sh` passes, and `dash -n install.sh` passes when dash is installed, confirming no bash-specific syntax was introduced.

criterion.title: No unrelated code changes
criterion.requirement: Only `install.sh` is modified during implementation of this plan; no backend/frontend runtime files are changed.
